aliases: กระชายเหลือง
crop.disambiguation_note: All sources verified to describe Boesenbergia rotunda (yellow/white fingerroot, the rhizome with yellow inner flesh used in Thai cuisine and traditional medicine). Sources about กระชายดำ (Kaempferia parviflora — black galangal/black ginger, a different genus and species) were explicitly EXCLUDED, including KUKR records, the krachai-dum DOA-Hort PDF, and the agricman.doae.go.th 2012 black-fingerroot guide. The Thai vernacular กระชาย, กระชายเหลือง, and กระชายขาว all refer to B. rotunda; กระชายดำ is a different species and is out of scope for this profile. Older taxonomic literature uses Boesenbergia pandurata (Roxb.) Schltr. as a synonym for B. rotunda.

sources.title: กรมส่งเสริมการเกษตร แนะวิธีปลูก "กระชาย" พืชสมุนไพรพื้นบ้านอย่างถูกวิธี
sources.publisher: Department of Agricultural Extension News Center (ศูนย์ข่าวกรมส่งเสริมการเกษตร), Ministry of Agriculture and Cooperatives
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly Boesenbergia rotunda (Zingiberaceae). Provides the most operationally complete Thai cultivation protocol (soil prep depth, seed-rhizome bud count, fungicide soak, spacing options, fertilizer schedule, harvest window 5-6 vs 8-9 months for peak compound concentration, lime-based rot management). This is the cornerstone Thai cultivation reference.

sources.title: หนุ่มนครปฐม ปลูกกระชายเป็นรายได้เสริม โกย 2-3 แสน/ไร่
sources.publisher: Phang-Nga Provincial Agriculture and Cooperatives Office (สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดพังงา) — Ministry of Agriculture and Cooperatives
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly กระชาย (yellow fingerroot), specifically the พันธุ์รากกล้วย cultivar (long-rooted), profiled through a 52-year-old Nakhon Pathom farmer (Khun Twi). NOT กระชายดำ. Provides realistic farmer-level economics and timing data for Central Thailand.

sources.title: 6 ประโยชน์ดีๆ ของกระชาย — สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดฉะเชิงเทรา
sources.publisher: Chachoengsao Provincial Agriculture and Cooperatives Office (สำนักงานเกษตรและสหกรณ์จังหวัดฉะเชิงเทรา)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: yellow fingerroot (Boesenbergia rotunda) traditional uses, explicitly distinguished by peppery-pungent flavor profile from the black variety. Useful for the 'uses' section of the profile.

sources.title: กระชาย — ศูนย์ปฏิบัติการวิจัยและเรือนปลูกพืชทดลอง คณะเกษตร กำแพงแสน
sources.publisher: Plant Conservation and Utilization Unit, Central Laboratory and Greenhouse Complex (ศูนย์ปฏิบัติการวิจัยและเรือนปลูกพืชทดลอง), Faculty of Agriculture at Kamphaengsaen, Kasetsart University
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: 'Boesenbergia rotunda (Linn.) Mansf.' explicit. University-curated herb resource page. Provides botanical/morphological detail and traditional-use overview.

sources.title: กระชาย — ฐานข้อมูลสมุนไพร คณะเภสัชศาสตร์ มหาวิทยาลัยอุบลราชธานี
sources.publisher: Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Ubon Ratchathani University (คณะเภสัชศาสตร์ มหาวิทยาลัยอุบลราชธานี)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicit 'Boesenbergia rotunda (L.) Mansf.' Faculty-of-Pharmacy crude-drug monograph. Critical for safety/toxicology section of the profile (favorable safety profile, 600 mg/kg rat micronucleus no-toxicity).

sources.title: กระชาย — สถานการแพทย์แผนไทยประยุกต์ คณะแพทยศาสตร์ มหาวิทยาลัยธรรมศาสตร์
sources.publisher: Applied Thai Traditional Medicine (สถานการแพทย์แผนไทยประยุกต์), Faculty of Medicine, Thammasat University
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: 'Boesenbergia pandurata (Roxb.) Schltr.' (older synonym for B. rotunda). University traditional-medicine reference. Useful for traditional-preparation specifics (5-10g decoction).

sources.title: Physico-Chemical Properties of Fingerroot (Boesenbergia rotunda) Crude Drug
sources.publisher: Journal of Thai Traditional and Alternative Medicine (วารสารการแพทย์แผนไทยและการแพทย์ทางเลือก), TCI-indexed
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicit 'Boesenbergia rotunda (L.) Mansf.' Thai peer-reviewed pharmacopoeial-quality study. Provides quantitative quality benchmarks (moisture, ash, volatile oil) for the rhizome as a crude drug.

sources.title: กระชาย: ชะลอความแก่ และบำรุงกำลัง — รศ.ดร.สุธาทิพ ภมรประวัติ
sources.publisher: Folk Doctor Foundation (มูลนิธิหมอชาวบ้าน), Thai Health Magazine, Vol. 315, July 2548 (2005)
sources.authors: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Sutatip Phamornpravat (รศ.ดร.สุธาทิพ ภมรประวัติ)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicit Boesenbergia rotunda. Named-expert authored, published by Folk Doctor Foundation (mulkonor mulnitihimochoaobon — long-running Thai public-health publisher; CLAUDE.md SOURCE_POLICY 'reputable Thai media' tier). Useful for everyday preparation forms and folk-safety framing.

sources.title: ปลูกกระชาย ทางรอดเกษตรกรยุคโควิด-19 — KUBOTA Agri Solutions
sources.publisher: Siam Kubota Corporation — KUBOTA (Agri) Solutions knowledge portal
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: explicitly กระชาย / Boesenbergia rotunda. Industry-affiliated media but content is operational and matches DOAE/OPSMOAC numbers. Treat as medium-confidence corroborating source for spacing and fertilizer schedule.

sources.title: Boesenbergia rotunda (L.) Mansf. — Plants of the World Online (Kew Science)
sources.publisher: Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew — Plants of the World Online
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: authoritative taxonomic reference. Confirms native range and accepted nomenclature (B. rotunda is current accepted name; B. pandurata used in older Thai literature is a synonym).

sources.title: Boesenbergia rotunda — Useful Tropical Plants
sources.publisher: Useful Tropical Plants Database (Ken Fern, Ajna Fern, 2014, CC BY-NC-SA 3.0)
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: tropical-context cultivation profile. Provides quantitative environmental envelope (temperature, rainfall, elevation, pH) and yield benchmarks (10-30 t/ha) directly applicable to Thai lowland conditions.

sources.title: Traditional usages, chemical metabolites, pharmacological activities, and pharmacokinetics of Boesenbergia rotunda (L.) Mansf.: a comprehensive review
sources.publisher: Frontiers in Pharmacology (March 2025), via PubMed Central
sources.verification_notes: WebFetch confirmed: most current comprehensive scientific review on B. rotunda (March 2025). Authoritative for chemistry, pharmacology, and the explicit caveat that comprehensive toxicology data remains incomplete — important for our SAFETY_POLICY framing.

research_notes: Disambiguation strict: every kept source is verified to describe Boesenbergia rotunda (yellow/white fingerroot). Sources about Kaempferia parviflora (กระชายดำ / black ginger / black galangal) — including the doa.go.th/hort 'การเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการผลิตพืชตระกูลกระชาย' PDF (focus on Kaempferia parviflora), agriman.doae.go.th/home/Research/Herb57/2012.pdf (krachai-dum), esc.doae.go.th 'การผลิตกระชายดำ.pdf', and the kukr.lib.ku.ac.th 'การผลิตและการจำหน่ายกระชายดำ' farm-economics study — were excluded because กระชายดำ is a different species (Kaempferia parviflora, not Boesenbergia rotunda).

Boesenbergia pandurata (Roxb.) Schltr. is treated as an older synonym for B. rotunda; the Thammasat ATTM source uses the older name but is unambiguously about the same species (yellow rhizome, same compounds, same indications).

Cultivation-data convergence is strong: DOAE news (10833), OPSMOAC Phang-Nga (Nakhon Pathom farmer), Kubota agri portal, and Useful Tropical Plants all converge on: loamy/sandy-loam well-drained soil; rhizome propagation; May-June planting; 5-9 month harvest window; yield in the 2-3 t/rai (≈12-19 t/ha) range; rot is the primary disease risk; lime-based pH adjustment for prevention.

Operational specificity: doaenews.doae.go.th/archives/10833 is the most operationally complete Thai source (tillage depth 25-30 cm, 3-5 buds per seed-rhizome, 30-min fungicide soak, 400 kg seed-rhizome per rai, 50 kg/rai ammonium sulfate timing, 200-300 kg/rai lime, 5-6 vs 8-9 month harvest windows). It anchors the cultivation section.

Safety/toxicology: UBU Pharmacy and Frontiers 2025 review converge — favorable acute-safety profile (UBU: no toxicity at 600 mg/kg in rat micronucleus assay; Frontiers: 700 mg/day extract deemed safe in South Korean studies) BUT comprehensive long-term toxicology data remains incomplete (Frontiers explicit). This is consistent with CLAUDE.md SAFETY_POLICY framing — culinary use is well-established and safe; medicinal-extract dosage claims should be Medium/Uncertain confidence.

Anti-SARS-CoV-2 angle (PMC7672115 / Nature Sci Rep 2020): kept implicit (panduratin A IC50 = 0.81 μM post-infection) but NOT cited as a primary source in the final list to keep total sources at 12 and to avoid encouraging health-claim drift in the Drafter. Frontiers 2025 review covers the antiviral angle in proper context.
Sources rejected during research:

- https://www.doa.go.th/pvp/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/กระชาย.pdf — curl returned 403; WebFetch returned binary/scanned PDF with no extractable text (Stage 4b fail), even though institutional credibility is high. Not citable per Stage 4b.
- https://ittm.dtam.moph.go.th/images/knowleaga/3/2กระชาย หน้า4-6.pdf — WebFetch returned undecodable binary; Stage 4b fail.
- https://huapluak.go.th/fileupload/2182722589.pdf — WebFetch returned undecodable binary; Stage 4b fail.

- https://www.kukr.lib.ku.ac.th/kukr_es/index.php/BKN_FRPD/search_detail/result/20008452 — TLS certificate altname error on www host; redirected canonical (kukr.lib.ku.ac.th/bkn/Detail/info/419941) loaded but body was metadata-only ('ไม่ได้รับสิทธิ์อนุญาตเผยแพร่' — access restricted), so Stage 4b body-content rule fails.
- http://agknowledge.arda.or.th/boesenbergiarotunda/?page_id=872 — ECONNREFUSED, fails Stage 4a.
- https://www.mdpi.com/2073-4395/13/6/1435 — curl 403 / WebFetch 403; Stage 4a fails for this session.
- https://www.researchgate.net/publication/371003109 — WebFetch 403; Stage 4a fails.
- https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33203926/ — fetched successfully and content-confirmed but excluded from final 12 to keep within 12-source cap; superseded by Frontiers 2025 review entry.

- https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5414452/ (Aphrodisiac Activity 2017) — fetched and content-confirmed but excluded from final 12 (12-cap; topic less relevant to public-good cultivation profile).
- https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3519102/ (2012 ethnomedicine review) — fetched and content-confirmed but excluded as superseded by Frontiers 2025 review.
- https://www.khaosod.co.th/technologychaoban/featured/article_187255 — curl 403 / WebFetch 403; Stage 4a fails for this session.
- https://www.khaosod.co.th/technologychaoban/featured/article_330505 — WebFetch 403; Stage 4a fails for this session.

- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boesenbergia_rotunda — fetched and content-confirmed but excluded from final 12 per Researcher quality bar (encyclopedic tertiary source; Kew POWO already covers taxonomy authoritatively).
- https://www.moac.go.th/agrivillage-dwl-files-441891791217 — .docx binary, Stage 4b fail.

Pipeline-friendly: every kept URL passes both curl HTTP-200 check (Stage 4a) and a WebFetch crop-specific body confirmation (Stage 4b). Nine of twelve sources are Thai-language or Thailand-context; three are international (Kew taxonomy, Useful Tropical Plants tropical cultivation envelope, Frontiers 2025 comprehensive scientific review). Seven sources are high-confidence (Thai government extension, Thai universities, Kew POWO, Thai-indexed peer-review, Frontiers 2025).
